task.name: Accessibility Review Task
task.objective: Review code and UI for WCAG 2.1 compliance

critical.mandate: Evaluate color contrast and screen reader support

step.title: Scope
step.action: Ask user for code/component to review

step.title: Semantic HTML and ARIA
step.action: Check for proper semantic HTML elements
Verify ARIA attributes, roles, and labels
Check for alt text on images, labels on form inputs
For each finding: cite the specific WCAG 2.1 success criterion (e.g., 1.1.1 Non-text Content, 1.3.1 Info and Relationships, 4.1.2 Name Role Value) and its conformance level (A/AA/AAA)

step.title: Keyboard and Focus
step.action: Check focus management and tab order
For each finding: cite the specific WCAG 2.1 success criterion (e.g., 2.1.1 Keyboard, 2.4.3 Focus Order, 2.4.1 Bypass Blocks) and its conformance level (A/AA/AAA)

step.title: Visual and Screen Reader
step.action: Evaluate color contrast ratios (4.5:1 for text, 3:1 for large text)
For each finding: cite the specific WCAG 2.1 success criterion (e.g., 1.4.3 Contrast Minimum, 1.3.3 Sensory Characteristics, 1.4.4 Resize Text) and its conformance level (A/AA/AAA)

step.title: Report
step.template-output: Generate accessibility review report organized by category (semantic HTML, ARIA, keyboard, focus, visual, screen reader). Each finding MUST use this format: WCAG Criterion ID (e.g., 1.1.1) | Criterion Name | Conformance Level (A/AA/AAA) | Severity (Critical/High/Medium/Low) | Finding Description | Remediation Guidance.

output.format: Markdown accessibility findings with WCAG criteria
